title: Call with Emily Stevens — m4a-audio orphan attachment
html:
TL;DR

  • April 16, 2026 Apple Note containing only a call recording attachment (m4a-audio reference, no transcript) titled "Call with Emily Stevens❤️‍🔥" — fire-heart emoji in title.
  • Pure audio-orphan sub-pattern: the call happened, the audio was saved as the note body, no follow-up text was written.
  • Same form as 2025 09 29 call recording, 2025 11 27 call with ethan hemstead, 2026 01 24 call with bapi, 2025 03 12 call recording — Yahir's call-recording-as-note habit is consistent across 2025–2026.
  • The fire-heart emoji is the load-bearing signal — it locates this call in the Emily arc continuum: 23 days after the March 24 misalignment audit (where Yahir documented value/worldview gaps), and 11 days before the vault was created. The emoji says the relationship is still emotionally lit despite the documented friction.
  • Captured ~40 minutes after the Tom Canchola contact as title note (two days earlier in calendar but same Apple-Notes session-cluster).

Why it matters

This note sits at the most-recent-edge of the Emily arc as the vault knows it. After the March 24 misalignment audit, a reader would expect either a breakup or a quiet fade; the fire-heart emoji on this April 16 capture argues for neither — the call happened, it mattered enough to record, the affect-marker was kept. Pair with the still-active April 27 build doc mentions of Emily in her entity page for the live-at-vault-creation status.

Privacy considerations

  • Audio file ($AF0CC7C0-…m4a-audio UUID-named attachment) is on-device only — not surfaced in this summary, not transcribed. The recording exists; its contents do not.
  • Recording a call without explicit transcript-publication is consistent with Yahir's pattern; no third-party PII surfaced here.

Open questions

  • Topic of the call? (Logistics, emotional, post-misalignment debrief?)
  • Was Emily aware of the recording? (recording as note convention question.)
  • Does the fire-heart contradict or coexist with the misalignment audit?
